name: Route of The Bridges – Ucieda Saja-Besaya Natural Park
name_original: Ruta de Los Puentes – Ucieda Parque Natural Saja-Besaya
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ruente offers a diverse landscape characterized by the valleys of the Saja and Bayones rivers, providing flatter areas for leisurely rides. Higher elevations present rolling hills and mountain pastures, with extensive forest tracks leading to panoramic viewpoints. A significant portion of the municipality lies within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, featuring dense forested areas and unique natural monuments. This varied terrain provides a mix of routes suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Ruente The most popular touring cycling route is Route of The Bridges – Ucieda Saja-Besaya Natural Park, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete, leading through the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ucieda Mountain House – Fallen Oak of Brujilices loop from Ruente, a difficult 21.3 miles (34.3 km) path, winding through forests and past the notable Fallen Oak of Brujilices. Local touring cyclists also love the Ancient Trees in the Forest loop from Ruente, a 11.7 miles (18.9 km) trail leading through ancient forests, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Ruente is defined by river valleys, extensive forest tracks, and rolling hills, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Ruente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ruente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ruente?
A: There are 14 touring cycling routes around Ruente, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 4 moderate routes, and 9 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ruente?
A: Ruente offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll encounter flatter areas in the valleys of the Saja and Bayones rivers, ideal for leisurely rides. As you ascend, the terrain transitions to rolling hills and mountain pastures, with extensive forest tracks leading to higher elevations. A significant portion of the routes are within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, featuring dense forested areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Ruente?
A: Yes, many routes in Ruente are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Mountain House – Fallen Oak of Brujilices loop from Ruente&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Trees in the Forest loop from Ruente&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate experience.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover along the cycling routes?
A: Ruente's routes offer access to several notable natural features and viewpoints. You can cycle towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt; for amazing landscapes and panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt;, a unique natural monument within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, is also accessible on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Mountain House – Fallen Oak of Brujilices loop from Ruente&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7428930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Monte Cabezón Sequoias Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt; offers another scenic stop.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Ruente?
A: While many routes in Ruente are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For families, routes with flatter sections along the Saja and Bayones river valleys might be more suitable. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile on komoot before heading out to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes in Ruente?
A: Many natural areas in Cantabria, including parts of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly, especially on trails where dogs are kept on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary. It's always best to check local signage or park rules for any restrictions regarding dogs on specific cycling paths or protected areas within Ruente.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ruente?
A: The region is popular for cycling in months like February and August, suggesting that both cooler and warmer periods can be enjoyable. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, while summer can be warm. Winter cycling is possible, but some higher elevation tracks might be affected by weather conditions.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful water features. The Lamiña Waterfalls, situated near Barcenillas, offer a beautiful spot within a beech forest, perfect for a relaxing stop. Additionally, La Fuentona de Ruente is a natural spring known for its intermittent water flow and local legends, providing a unique point of interest.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ruente?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ruente are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to forest tracks, and the immersive natural experience within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. The variety of routes, including options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012454693/route-of-the-bridges-ucieda-saja-besaya-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of The Bridges – Ucieda Saja-Besaya Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy touring cycling route available around Ruente. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Always check the route details on komoot for specific information on distance and elevation.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Ruente?
A: While specific parking locations for each route are not detailed here, Ruente is a municipality with various access points to its natural areas. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas near trailheads or in the villages like Ruente or Ucieda, especially when planning to access routes within the Saja-Besaya Natural Park.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Ruente and its surrounding villages offer traditional mountain architecture and amenities. While specific cafes on every route are not listed, you can often find establishments in the towns you pass through or near the start/end points of your ride. Planning your route to include stops in villages like Ruente or Ucieda will allow for refreshment breaks.
tips: There are several recreational areas with tables and barbecues, almost all with grills. There is also a huge children's playground and a bar that is open in summer. It has bathrooms, independent of the opening hours of the bar. Along the route of the Bayones River you will find the Ucieda pools where you can bathe.

The Oak "Tumbao" is one of the trees cataloged within "singular trees of Cantabria" and it is a "Cajiga" (Quercus Robur) about 25 meters high and approximately 250 years old, its curiosity is that one day full of strong wind blew snow on its branches and the tree fell because it could not withstand so much weight, but instead of being its end and by keeping some of its roots rooted in the ground, it was able to survive and new branches sprouted. Today 4 of its branches are 4 rootless trees formed from the original trunk and filling Brujilices with beauty, within the Vados River Forest.
A beautiful Beech specimen of more than 35 meters in height, included in the catalog of singular trees of Cantabria (specifically tree nº20)
